assetUi.notFoundText: It seems you knocked on the wrong door... or maybe this room is still a secret!

splashScreen.title: Ca' Perenzin
splashScreen.text: Your relaxing experience is being prepared

hero.heroIntroOne: Silence here is not an absence, but a presence in itself.
hero.heroIntroTwo: Leave the urban noise at the entrance and give yourself the luxury of finding your own natural rhythm.

heroOpen.headline: The lost art of stopping.
heroOpen.content: No schedules, no notifications, no rush. Just you, your rhythm, and nature.
heroOpen.textLeft: Luxury is not in things, but in presence.

textApop: You will arrive with the city's clamor still clinging to you. The first impression may be almost a shock: no cars, no sirens—just a sanctuary where time appears to have stood still. Give yourself two hours. That is how long it takes for your breath to sync with the rhythm of the hills. Then, the magic happens: you will stop rushing and begin to truly feel.
textSectionB: True luxury is stopping to gaze at something that changes constantly while remaining still. At the core of Ca'Perenzin lies the fireplace. Forget the glare of screens or ambient noise; here, only the warm glow of the fire dances, reshaping the shadows around you.
subHeadlineD: Many dread the deep dark of the hills. What becomes of us without the city lights?
noteD: You won't be bored. You will rediscover the art of speaking softly and holding hands.

star.headline: The Infinite
star.content: The answer lies above you. Without light pollution, the sky returns to its ancient state: a carpet of stars so sharp you will feel as if you are falling into them.

moon.headline: The Silver
moon.content: On full moon nights, the valley lights up with a silver glow so powerful you can walk without torches.

rain.headline: The Rhythm
rain.content: This is when the shelter becomes a den. The sound of water on the roof becomes a hypnotic lullaby, a rhythm that induces the deepest sleep you have ever known.

sectionF.paragraphOne: No electronic 'beeps'. Your awakening has only one sound: the slow gurgle of the Moka pot rising. And it has only one scent: that of a freshly baked cake, smelling of butter, sugar, and patience.
sectionF.paragraphTwo: In the small lounge, the fireplace is not a piece of furniture, it is a companion. Its warmth is not the dry air of an air conditioner, but an embrace that wraps around your shoulders. It is the breakfast of your childhood. Simple, homemade, infinitely good.

content.paragraphOne: You will find nothing here that hasn't touched the soil of our hills. The morning begins with the imperfect and sincere texture of our tarts, kneaded by hand while you are still sleeping.
content.paragraphTwo: Savor 'Mother Earth': eggs that taste of sun, fruit that follows only the calendar of the seasons, and Perenzin cheeses that tell ancient stories. It is a clean, healthy taste, devoid of preservatives.
content.paragraphThree: It is the honest flavor of the past, the kind that nourishes not just the body, but the memory.

content.paragraphOne: On mornings when the air outside bites, in here time slows down to a halt. Our lounge with the fireplace is not a simple space: it is a shelter within a shelter. Imagine the crackling of wood as the only soundtrack.
content.paragraphTwo: The heat coloring your cheeks. An infusion of positive energy in your hands. It is a primitive and rare luxury: enjoying the silence while the fire dances, feeling finally safe, protected from the whole world.

sectionI.headline: Beyond the gate, a world of magic.

sectionL.content: To understand these hills, it is not enough to watch them from a window: you must breathe them. Smell the cut grass, feel the temperature drop in the shade of a wood, hear the sound of gravel under your wheels. With our E-Bikes, climbs become gentle plains and the landscape enters you. The true luxury? No logistics.

cardOne.headline: Adrenaline and Peaks
cardOne.content: From the Revine lakes where kitesurfers fly, to the ancient forests of Cansiglio and the peaks of the Dolomites. For those seeking the deep breath of happy fatigue.

cardTwo.headline: Hidden Treasures
cardTwo.content: Medieval villages like Cison and Follina, thousand-year-old castles and secret panoramic points like the Giant Bench. We will reveal the corners only locals know.

cardThree.headline: Soul and Taste
cardThree.content: Not just Prosecco. A journey through historic wineries, award-winning Perenzin cheeses and small artisan shops. For those who want to taste the history of these hills.

sectionN.headline: Far from the world, close to everything.

info.title: The fast route with Airlink
info.text: Canova airport is tiny and super convenient. Just outside the terminal, look for the Treviso Airlink (the red bus). It is a dedicated service taking you directly to Central Station with no stops. Once on the train, you will be in Conegliano in just 15 minutes.

info.title: The smoothest way from the Lagoon
info.text: From Marco Polo Airport, avoid entering Venice island. The secret is taking the ATVO Fly Bus shuttle directly to Venice Mestre station (Mainland). From there, trains to Conegliano leave every 20 minutes and get you to your destination in less than half an hour.

infoBox.title: THE WATER SALON
infoBox.text: Often underestimated, Treviso is a jewel of canals and frescoed arcades that we locals love deeply. It is the perfect destination for a relaxing afternoon:
dotList: What to do: Get lost in the Buranelli canals, shop in the downtown boutiques and taste the real Tiramisù (born here!).
How to go: Forget the car and parking. From Conegliano station a train leaves about every 20 minutes. In less than half an hour you'll be in the center, ready for an aperitif.

buttons.label: Arrived by train?
buttons.text: If your departure city doesn't have a direct line to Conegliano, Treviso Centrale is a very convenient hub. Connections to Conegliano are fast and frequent until late evening.

infoBox.title: WORRY-FREE VENICE
infoBox.text: Many tourists make the mistake of driving to Venice, ending up trapped in traffic and paying crazy sums for Piazzale Roma parking. The winning strategy is starting from here:
dotList: The right move: Leave your car comfortably at Conegliano station (15 min from us).
The journey: Enjoy 45 minutes of relaxation on the train.
The magical arrival: You will get off at Santa Lucia station, which overlooks the Grand Canal directly. No bus, no transfer. You are already inside the postcard.

buttons.label: The timing trick
buttons.text: Catch the fast regional train at 9:02 or 9:30. You will arrive with the perfect morning light, avoiding the dawn commuter rush. And for the return? Trains run until late evening, enjoy dinner there.

infoBox.title: THE CONEGLIANO HUB
infoBox.text: Conegliano station is the real logistical hub of the area. Just 15 minutes by car from the B&B, it is served by all main trains (Fast Regionals and Frecce).
dotList: Parking: Large and convenient right in front of the tracks.
Transfer: Don't have a car? Taxis are always available in the square, or ask us for our trusted driver's contact.

sectionO.headline: Ca'Perenzin
sectionO.adress: Località Rive di S. Pietro, 10, 31020 Tarzo TV

contacts.headline: Direct Line
contacts.content: Whether it is to plan a surprise, ask for details about the road or simply for a greeting before arrival, we are here. At Ca'Perenzin no call centers answer. The family answers.

infoBox.adress: Località Rive di S. Pietro, 10, 31020 Tarzo TV

dataRooms.carousellContent: Our highest dwelling, with wooden ceilings warming the atmosphere. Generous spaces and a dominant view of the Visentin, to gather those you love in a single embrace.
dataRooms.description: The heart is the organ that never rests, the one that gives rhythm to life. This room was designed with the same principle: to be the vital center of your stay. Wider and more generous in spaces, the Heart Room is thought for sharing. It is the place where laughter bounces off the walls, where you meet at the end of the day to tell each other the discoveries made in the hills. Here architecture favors union without sacrificing freedom of movement. It is a warm, vibrant space, where you feel like family — however you define this word.

feedback.label: They say about us
feedback.headline: Ca'Perenzin
feedback.content: This room is still a blank page. The silence is intact, the light awaits. Be the first to write the beginning of this story.

dataRooms.carousellContent: An alcove facing east. Waking up with the first sun caressing the natural wood. Ideal for those seeking mental clarity.
dataRooms.description:
There is a precise moment, just before the world wakes up, when the air has a different scent. The Breath Room was designed to capture that instant. Exposed to welcome the first light of day, this room is not just a place to sleep, but a space to expand the chest and slow the heartbeat. 

Here colors are light, fabrics impalpable and the boundary between inside and outside blurs. It is the perfect alcove for those seeking mental clarity, for those who want to start the day with a deep, clean, absolute breath.

feedback.label: They say about us
feedback.headline: Ca'Perenzin
feedback.content: This room is still a blank page. The silence is intact, the light awaits. Be the first to write the beginning of this story.

dataRooms.carousellContent: Ground floor, in direct contact with matter. A shelter stripped of technological superfluity, designed for total digital disconnection and deep sleep.
dataRooms.description: There are places made for looking out, and others designed for looking in. The Soul Room belongs to this second, rare category. It is a gathered, dense space, where the outside world stops knocking at the door. Here architecture becomes an embrace: warm materials, muffled acoustics and an atmosphere that naturally invites calm. It is the perfect room for those who need to stop. To read that book waiting for months, to write, or simply to find that inner voice that the daily routine has covered with noise.

dataRooms.carousellContent: Freedom of movement and absence of architectural barriers. A bright triple suite on the ground floor, where comfort embraces every family need.
dataRooms.description: There are places made for looking out, and others designed for looking in. The Soul Room belongs to this second, rare category. experience.headline: KiteSurf at the Lakes
experience.content: Here, the clock is useless; you just need to feel the air. Punctual every afternoon, the thermal wind transforms this turquoise mirror of water into an arena of pure adrenaline. Lake Santa Croce is the natural gym for kitesurfers: flat water, constant wind, and the Alpago peaks framing you. Whether you are experts ready to jump or beginners wanting to learn to fly on water, this is your destination.

tips.contentOne: The best wind usually picks up after 1:00 PM.
tips.contentTwo: The VKC school is located in the Farra d'Alpago/Poiatte area, excellent for equipment rental.

experience.headline: Swing in the Clouds
experience.content: Forget golf courses manicured like urban gardens. Here, you play with wild nature. At 1,000 meters altitude, the Cansiglio Golf Club is considered one of the most beautiful mountain courses in Europe. A natural 'Links' course where holes hide among karst sinkholes and fairways are walls of centuries-old beech trees. The air is crisp even in August; the silence is broken only by the sound of the driver. A technical challenge for players, a walk in beauty for companions.

tips.contentTwo: Polo/golf attire required (it is a historic and respected club).

experience.headline: The Alpine Beat
experience.content: The mountain is not just to be watched, but listened to. Dolomiti Beat is your pass to the peaks. A team of professional guides ready to take you where normal trails don't reach: from the ancient forests of Cansiglio to the spires of the Dolomites. Whether it's a sunrise trek, a snowshoe hike under the full moon, or an adrenaline-filled E-Bike outing, with them exploration is safe, narrated, and unforgettable. No maps needed, just the desire to walk.

tips.contentOne: They have a fixed calendar but also organize private outings on request.
tips.contentTwo: They often provide snowshoes or poles.

experience.headline: The Yellow Giant
experience.content: Returning to being children for a moment. This is the promise of Big Bench #181, a huge sun-yellow bench overlooking the two lakes of Revine. Sitting here, with dangling legs not touching the ground, changes your perspective on the world. Below you, the mirror of water; in front, the amphitheater of the Prealps. Expert tip: On clear days, sharpen your sight towards the hills to the west: you might spot the profile of the mountain where the Winged Lion you just visited rests.

tips.contentOne: If guests have the 'Big Bench Passport', they can get it stamped in local village premises.
tips.contentTwo: Perfect for aperitif time (bringing a bottle in your backpack).

experience.headline: The Mirror of the Prealps
experience.content: While the peaks challenge the sky, the Revine Lakes reflect it. Two water twins of glacial origin where time slows down. Here the experience is double: you can rent a pedal boat and dive into the swimmable waters, or travel back in time visiting the Livelet Archaeological Park, a stilt village suspended on water like 6,000 years ago. The perfect place for a lake-view picnic, far from the crowd.

tips.contentOne: Rent a pedal boat at sunset to see the mountains turn pink from the water.

experience.headline: The Winged Guardian
experience.content: Where the storm destroyed, art rebuilt. On the summit of Monte Simoni awaits the world's largest Winged Lion, sculpted entirely from the wood of trees felled by cyclone Vaia. It is not just a statue; it is a pilgrimage. It is reached via a steep path among the vineyards, but the reward is double: coming face to face with the majesty of 1,500 wooden fragments and dominating the entire arc of the UNESCO hills with a single glance.

tips.contentOne: Ideal at sunset for the golden light on the wood.

experience.headline: The Living Postcard
experience.content: It looks like a painting, but it is all real. The Molinetto della Croda is a jewel of 17th-century rural architecture, carved into the bare rock and caressed by a waterfall. Here, time has the slow rhythm of the stone millstone which, still today, transforms corn into golden flour. It is the place where the millers' toil became poetry. Visiting it means entering the ancient heart of our hills, amidst the scent of flour and the sound of water.

tips.contentOne: They often sell flour ground on-site.
tips.contentTwo: Very beautiful nature trails depart from here along the Lierza stream.

experience.headline: SPA Castel Brando
experience.content: Imagine immersing yourself in hot water surrounded by ancient walls. The Princess SPA of CastelBrando is not a simple wellness center, but a journey through time. Located inside one of the oldest castles in Europe, it offers 2,000 sqm of relaxation where history meets steam: ancient Roman baths, saunas, counter-current swimming pool, and a Himalayan salt cave. Here, you don't just relax as guests, but as nobles of the court.

tips.contentTwo: There is also a private 'Spa Suite' for couples.

experience.headline: The Cloister of Silence
experience.content: If silence had an architectural form, it would be the cloister of Follina. In this Cistercian masterpiece of the 12th century, the stone seems to have been embroidered like lace. Walking along the quadriporticus, observing the play of light between the columns, all different from each other, is a form of moving meditation. A place where time stops, perfect for finding that inner peace we often seek in vain elsewhere.

tips.contentTwo: Visits to the cloister and basilica are usually free.

experience.headline: The Giant of the Hills
experience.content: Dominating the horizon for nearly a thousand years. San Salvatore is not just a castle; it is a living fortress of the Collalto family. A double circle of walls embraces palaces and hanging gardens, offering a dive into history and a view that, on clear days, reaches as far as Venice.

tips.contentOne: Check openings: it is a private residence visible only on specific dates or by reservation.
tips.contentTwo: If closed, the Conte Collalto Winery at the foot of the fortress will welcome you with the same nobility.

experience.headline: The White Gold
experience.content: If cheese is a religion, this is its sanctuary. For five generations, the Perenzin family has been transforming milk into world-award-winning art. Forget the old dairy shop: the 'PER' is a modern Cheese Bar where tasting is a sensory journey. Here, a cheese excites as much as a great wine.

tips.contentOne: Don't miss the 'San Pietro in beeswax', a masterpiece.
tips.contentTwo: Book a guided tasting to understand the secrets of affinage.

experience.headline: The Soul of Wine
experience.content: You don't come here just to drink, but to understand. The Antoniazzi family cultivates heroic vineyards on impossible slopes to give you a Prosecco DOCG that has character to spare. Their winery is a glass-walled lounge immersed in greenery, where wine is narrated with passion. A tasting experience that explains, better than a thousand words, what 'heroic viticulture' means.

tips.contentOne: Do not miss their goose salami and 'Oca in onto', a true Slow Food presidium.
tips.contentTwo: Ask to taste their reds as well, rare for this area.

experience.headline: The Stone Lounge
experience.content: A 19th-century farmhouse that has changed its skin. Ca' Piadera combines the raw stone of Nogarolo with contemporary and refined design. Here, Venetian cuisine wears its Sunday best: hand-rolled pasta and garden flavors served with surprising elegance. It is the perfect refuge for those seeking the substance of tradition without sacrificing style.

tips.contentOne: In summer, book in the 'brolo' to dine among the vine rows.
tips.contentTwo: Try their gourmet 'cicchetti' with the house Prosecco.

experience.headline: The Keeper of the Fire
experience.content: Since 1919, this has been the steadfast point of local cuisine. At Ai Pini, they don't follow trends; they honor memory. The heart of the room is the ancient open fireplace ('larin'), where meat is still cooked over live embers right before your eyes. It is the Sunday family restaurant, with reassuring flavors: from 'soppressa' with polenta to salt cod, to mixed grills smelling of wood smoke. An honest place, where hospitality has been managed by the same family for three generations.

tips.contentOne: If you love meat, their fireplace-grilled selection is essential (ask for a table near the fire in winter).
tips.contentTwo: In the warm season, book under the outdoor pergola: you dine in the cool air with a relaxing view of the hills.

experience.headline: The Invisible Host
experience.content: An Iconic Experience! Here, the host is the landscape itself. In this farmhouse perched among the most precious vineyards in the world (the Cartizze), the ancient rule of trust prevails. You enter, serve yourself by taking a bottle and a platter from the fridge, leave your offering in the box, and go out to enjoy the show. There are no assigned chairs: your table is a tree trunk or a vine row facing infinity. It is the apotheosis of Venetian freedom: bread, wine, and a breathtaking horizon.

tips.contentOne: Parking is down in the valley: be prepared for a short but steep uphill walk among the vineyards.
tips.contentTwo: The best moment is the golden hour at sunset, when the Cartizze hills are literally dyed with light.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
intro: This Privacy Policy describes the policies of Ca'Perenzin, Località Rive di S. Pietro 10, Treviso 31020, Italy, email: tatiana.perenzin@gmail.com, phone: +39 3333597947 on the collection, use and disclosure of your information that we collect when you use our website ( https://caperenzin.it ). (the “Service”). By accessing or using the Service, you are consenting to the collection, use and disclosure of your information in accordance with this Privacy Policy. If you do not consent to the same, please do not access or use the Service.

We may modify this Privacy Policy at any time without any prior notice to you and will post the revised Privacy Policy on the Service. The revised Policy will be effective 180 days from when the revised Policy is posted in the Service and your continued access or use of the Service after such time will constitute your acceptance of the revised Privacy Policy. We therefore recommend that you periodically review this page.

sections.title: Information We Collect:
sections.content: We will collect and process the following personal information about you:

sections.title: How We Use Your Information:
sections.content: We will use the information that we collect about you for the following purposes:
sections.extraContent: If we want to use your information for any other purpose, we will ask you for consent and will use your information only on receiving your consent and then, only for the purpose(s) for which grant consent unless we are required to do otherwise by law.

sections.title: How We Share Your Information:
sections.content: We will not transfer your personal information to any third party without seeking your consent, except in limited circumstances as described below:
sections.extraContent: We require such third party’s to use the personal information we transfer to them only for the purpose for which it was transferred and not to retain it for longer than is required for fulfilling the said purpose. We may also disclose your personal information for the following: (1) to comply with applicable law, regulation, court order or other legal process; (2) to enforce your agreements with us, including this Privacy Policy; or (3) to respond to claims that your use of the Service violates any third-party rights. If the Service or our company is merged or acquired with another company, your information will be one of the assets that is transferred to the new owner.

sections.title: Retention Of Your Information:
sections.content: We will retain your personal information with us for 10 years or for as long as we need it to fulfill the purposes for which it was collected as detailed in this Privacy Policy. We may need to retain certain information for longer periods such as record-keeping / reporting in accordance with applicable law or for other legitimate reasons like enforcement of legal rights, fraud prevention, etc. Residual anonymous information and aggregate information, neither of which identifies you (directly or indirectly), may be stored indefinitely.

sections.title: Your Rights:
sections.content: Depending on the law that applies, you may have a right to access and rectify or erase your personal data or receive a copy of your personal data, restrict or object to the active processing of your data, ask us to share (port) your personal information to another entity, withdraw any consent you provided to us to process your data, a right to lodge a complaint with a statutory authority and such other rights as may be relevant under applicable laws. To exercise these rights, you can write to us at tatiana.perenzin@gmail.com. We will respond to your request in accordance with applicable law.
sections.extraContent: Do note that if you do not allow us to collect or process the required personal information or withdraw the consent to process the same for the required purposes, you may not be able to access or use the services for which your information was sought.

sections.title: Cookies, etc.
sections.content: To learn more about how we use these and your choices in relation to these tracking technologies, please refer to our Cookie Policy.

sections.title: Security:
sections.content: The security of your information is important to us and we will use reasonable security measures to prevent the loss, misuse or unauthorized alteration of your information under our control. However, given the inherent risks, we cannot guarantee absolute security and consequently, we cannot ensure or warrant the security of any information you transmit to us and you do so at your own risk.

sections.title: Third Party Links & Use Of Your Information:
sections.content: Our Service may contain links to other websites that are not operated by us. This Privacy Policy does not address the privacy policy and other practices of any third parties, including any third party operating any website or service that may be accessible via a link on the Service. We strongly advise you to review the privacy policy of every site you visit. We have no control over and assume no responsibility for the content, privacy policies or practices of any third party sites or services.

sections.title: Grievance / Data Protection Officer:
sections.content: If you have any queries or concerns about the processing of your information that is available with us, you may email our Grievance Officer at Ca'Perenzin, Località Rive di S. Pietro 10, email: tatiana.perenzin@gmail.com. We will address your concerns in accordance with applicable law.

sections.title: 3. USER REPRESENTATIONS
sections.content:
By using the Services, you represent and warrant that: (1) you have the legal capacity and you agree to comply with these Legal Terms; (2) you are not a minor in the jurisdiction in which you reside, or if a minor, you have received parental permission to use the Services; (3) you will not access the Services through automated or non-human means, whether through a bot, script or otherwise; (4) you will not use the Services for any illegal or unauthorized purpose; and (5) your use of the Services will not violate any applicable law or regulation.
If you provide any information that is untrue, inaccurate, not current, or incomplete, we have the right to suspend or terminate your account and refuse any and all current or future use of the Services (or any portion thereof).

sections.title: 4. PURCHASES AND PAYMENT
sections.content:
We accept the following forms of payment:
- Visa
- Mastercard
- American Express
- Discover
- PayPal
You agree to provide current, complete, and accurate purchase and account information for all purchases made via the Services. You further agree to promptly update account and payment information, including email address, payment method, and payment card expiration date, so that we can complete your transactions and contact you as needed. Sales tax will be added to the price of purchases as deemed required by us. We may change prices at any time. All payments shall be in Euros.
You agree to pay all charges at the prices then in effect for your purchases and any applicable shipping fees, and you authorize us to charge your chosen payment provider for any such amounts upon placing your order. We reserve the right to correct any errors or mistakes in pricing, even if we have already requested or received payment.
We reserve the right to refuse any order placed through the Services. We may, in our sole discretion, limit or cancel quantities purchased per person, per household, or per order. These restrictions may include orders placed by or under the same customer account, the same payment method, and/or orders that use the same billing or shipping address. We reserve the right to limit or prohibit orders that, in our sole judgment, appear to be placed by dealers, resellers, or distributors.

sections.title: 5. POLICY
sections.content: All sales are final and no refund will be issued.

sections.title: 6. PROHIBITED ACTIVITIES
sections.content:
You may not access or use the Services for any purpose other than that for which we make the Services available. The Services may not be used in connection with any commercial endeavors except those that are specifically endorsed or approved by us.
As a user of the Services, you agree not to:
Systematically retrieve data or other content from the Services to create or compile, directly or indirectly, a collection, compilation, database, or directory without written permission from us.
Trick, defraud, or mislead us and other users, especially in any attempt to learn sensitive account information such as user passwords.
Circumvent, disable, or otherwise interfere with security-related features of the Services, including features that prevent or restrict the use or copying of any Content or enforce limitations on the use of the Services and/or the Content contained therein.
Disparage, tarnish, or otherwise harm, in our opinion, us and/or the Services.
Use any information obtained from the Services in order to harass, abuse, or harm another person.
Make improper use of our support services or submit false reports of abuse or misconduct.
Use the Services in a manner inconsistent with any applicable laws or regulations.
Engage in unauthorized framing of or linking to the Services.
Upload or transmit (or attempt to upload or to transmit) viruses, Trojan horses, or other material, including excessive use of capital letters and spamming (continuous posting of repetitive text), that interferes with any party’s uninterrupted use and enjoyment of the Services or modifies, impairs, disrupts, alters, or interferes with the use, features, functions, operation, or maintenance of the Services.
Engage in any automated use of the system, such as using scripts to send comments or messages, or using any data mining, robots, or similar data gathering and extraction tools.
Delete the copyright or other proprietary rights notice from any Content.
Attempt to impersonate another user or person or use the username of another user.
Upload or transmit (or attempt to upload or to transmit) any material that acts as a passive or active information collection or transmission mechanism, including without limitation, clear graphics interchange formats ("gifs"), 1×1 pixels, web bugs, cookies, or other similar devices (sometimes referred to as "spyware" or "passive collection mechanisms" or "pcms").
Interfere with, disrupt, or create an undue burden on the Services or the networks or services connected to the Services.
Harass, annoy, intimidate, or threaten any of our employees or agents engaged in providing any portion of the Services to you.
Attempt to bypass any measures of the Services designed to prevent or restrict access to the Services, or any portion of the Services.
Copy or adapt the Services' software, including but not limited to Flash, PHP, HTML, JavaScript, or other code.
Except as permitted by applicable law, decipher, decompile, disassemble, or reverse engineer any of the software comprising or in any way making up a part of the Services.
Except as may be the result of standard search engine or Internet browser usage, use, launch, develop, or distribute any automated system, including without limitation, any spider, robot, cheat utility, scraper, or offline reader that accesses the Services, or use or launch any unauthorized script or other software.
Use a buying agent or purchasing agent to make purchases on the Services.
Make any unauthorized use of the Services, including collecting usernames and/or email addresses of users by electronic or other means for the purpose of sending unsolicited email, or creating user accounts by automated means or under false pretenses.
Use the Services as part of any effort to compete with us or otherwise use the Services and/or the Content for any revenue-generating endeavor or commercial enterprise.
Make speculative, false, or fraudulent bookings.

Any use of the Services in violation of the foregoing violates these Legal Terms and may result in, among other things, termination or suspension of your rights to use the Services.

sections.content: We care about data privacy and security. Please review our Privacy Policy: http://caperenzin.it/policy. sections.content: If any complaint with us is not satisfactorily resolved, you can contact the Complaint Assistance Unit of the Division of Consumer Services of the California Department of Consumer Affairs in writing at 1625 North Market Blvd., Suite N 112, Sacramento, California 95834 or by telephone at (800) 952-5210 or (916) 445-1254.

sections.title: 24. TOURIST TAX:
sections.content: The tourist tax is not included in the total price and must be paid in cash upon arrival, according to local municipal regulations.

sections.title: 25. CHECK-IN/CHECK-OUT:
sections.content: Check-in is available from 3:00 PM to 8:00 PM. Check-out must be completed by 10:30 AM.

sections.title: 26. PETS:
sections.content: Pets are welcome but must be supervised at all times. Any damage caused by pets will be the sole responsibility of the owner.

sections.title: 27. SMOKING:
sections.content: Smoking is strictly prohibited inside the rooms and common indoor areas.

sections.title: 28. EXTRA SERVICES:
sections.content: Use of cradles or extra beds must be requested and confirmed in advance.
